intro: Oschatzia saxifraga, also known as Saxifraga oppositifolia, is a small, flowering plant that belongs to the Saxifragaceae family, commonly found in alpine regions. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine due to its potential health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help to reduce stress, improve cardiovascular health, and protect against certain diseases. The bioactive constituents of Oschatzia saxifraga include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
